{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[渋谷区]][[渋谷]]1-12-16 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = |創建 = [[元亀]]元年（[[1570年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 宮益御嶽神社、渋谷御嶽神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|鳥居と拝殿（遠景）]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|南方の[[宮益坂]]から伸びる参道]] '''御嶽神社'''（みたけじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[渋谷区]]の[[神社]]。当神社が名の由来となっている[[宮益坂]]の名を採って'''宮益御嶽神社'''（みやます みたけじんじゃ）とも称される。 渋谷区立商工会館/消費者センターが入る建物の2階屋上に南向きで鎮座しており、南方の宮益坂に向かって緩やかに参道の階段が伸びている。 == 概要 == [[1570年]]（[[元亀]]元年）に創建された。 [[境内]]には「明治天皇御嶽神社御小休所阯」の記念碑がある。[[明治]]3年[[4月17日 (旧暦)|4月17日]]（[[1870年]][[5月17日]]）、[[明治天皇]]は駒場野練兵場（現[[東京大学駒場地区キャンパス]]）で行われた[[大日本帝国陸軍|陸軍]]の[[軍事演習]]を統監するに際し、皇城（現[[皇居]]）から駒場野練兵場までの間の休憩所を当社に置いた。この「聖蹟」（明治天皇ゆかりの地）となった栄誉を記念した石碑である佐藤昇 著『渋谷区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド13)』[[学生社]]、1992年、112-114p。 当社の[[境内]]では、毎年[[11月]]の酉の日に[[酉の市]]が開かれている。「三の酉」がある年は、火災が多いといわれている。 2023年4月12日に都市計画決定により取り壊される予定がある{{Cite web |title=渋谷駅東口「宮益坂地区」大規模再開発の都市計画決定 大山街道再整備も |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=シブヤ経済新聞 |access-date=2023-12-05}}。御嶽神社が含まれる再開発（[[宮益坂地区再開発]]）では再開発に対する反対活動も行われている。 == ギャラリー == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|一ノ鳥居 mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|二ノ鳥居 mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|狛犬（左・吽形） mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|狛犬（右・阿形） mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|境内から南を見る。中央・奥のビルは[[渋谷ヒカリエ]] ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|神社の裏側（北側）。渋谷区立商工会館と消費者センターが入る。 == 交通アクセス == *[[渋谷駅]]より徒歩3分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *佐藤昇 著『渋谷区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド13)』[[学生社]]、1992年 == 外部リンク == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 宮益御嶽神社] - 渋谷宮益商店街 {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みたけしんしや しふやく}} [[Category:渋谷区の神社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:渋谷]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Infobox shrine | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|ja|渋谷}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} | founded = {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 3, {{ill|May 17|ja|5月17日}}, {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|ja|皇居}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|ja|東京大学駒場地区キャンパス}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}} {{ill|military exercise|ja|軍事演習}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival held on {{ill|November|ja|11月}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|ja|渋谷ヒカリエ}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines|ja|Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:qq|ja|Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:11|ja|Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing|ja|Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=draft}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|ja|渋谷ヒカリエ|12=draft}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. {{Infobox shrine | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|ja|渋谷|qq=draft}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 3, {{ill|May 17|ja|5月17日|qq=draft}}, {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|ja|皇居|qq=draft}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|ja|東京大学駒場地区キャンパス|qq=draft}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|qq=draft}} {{ill|military exercise|ja|軍事演習|qq=draft}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft}} festival held on {{ill|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|ja|渋谷ヒカリエ|qq=draft}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|qq=draft}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines|ja|Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing|ja|Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]]

The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|ja|渋谷ヒカリエ|qq=draft}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|qq=draft}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines|ja|Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing|ja|Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|ja|渋谷|qq=draft}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 3, {{ill|May 17|ja|5月17日|qq=draft}}, {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|ja|皇居|qq=draft}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|ja|東京大学駒場地区キャンパス|qq=draft}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|qq=draft}} {{ill|military exercise|ja|軍事演習|qq=draft}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft}} festival held on {{ill|November|ja|11月|qq=draft}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|ja|渋谷ヒカリエ|qq=draft}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|qq=draft}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines|ja|Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing|ja|Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{Infobox shrine | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|ja|渋谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya (district)|qq=}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genki|qq=}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|ja|1570th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genki|qq=}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|qq=}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 3, {{ill|May 17|ja|5月17日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 17|qq=}}, {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Meiji|qq=}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|ja|皇居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|qq=}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|ja|東京大学駒場地区キャンパス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|qq=}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Army|qq=}} {{ill|military exercise|ja|軍事演習|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military exercise|qq=}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on {{ill|November|ja|11月|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November|qq=}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|ja|渋谷ヒカリエ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Hikarie|qq=}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Station|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines|ja|Category:Shibuya Ward Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing|ja|Category:mass imported Shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|ja|渋谷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya (district)|qq=}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|1570|ja|1570年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genki|qq=}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shibuya|ja|渋谷区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|ja|1570年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1570|qq=}} ({{ill|Genki|ja|元亀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genki|qq=}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|qq=}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|ja|4月17日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 17|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 3, {{ill|May 17|ja|5月17日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 17|qq=}}, {{ill|1870|ja|1870年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Meiji|qq=}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|ja|皇居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|qq=}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|ja|東京大学駒場地区キャンパス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|qq=}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Japanese Army|qq=}} {{ill|military exercise|ja|軍事演習|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military exercise|qq=}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on {{ill|November|ja|11月|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November|qq=}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|ja|渋谷ヒカリエ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Hikarie|qq=}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|ja|渋谷駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Station|qq=}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya (district)]] 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | founded = [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|qq=}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)区の神社]]

The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|qq=}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[sn:Mitake Shrine (Shibuya Ward)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)区の神社]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}}

The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|qq=|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)区の神社]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852577]] Wikidata name: Mitake Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya (district)]] 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | founded = [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|qq=|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|qq=|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|qq=|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}}

The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852577]] Wikidata name: Mitake Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya (district)]] 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | founded = [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852577]] Wikidata name: Mitake Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya (district)]] 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | founded = [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852577}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852577]] Wikidata name: Mitake Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya (district)]] 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | founded = [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q106852577}}

The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site."
