The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Shibuya (district)|Shibuya (district)]] 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | founded = [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Shibuya|Shibuya]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in [[:en:1570|1570]] ([[:en:Genki (era)|Genki]] 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On [[:en:April 17|April 17]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3, [[:en:May 17|May 17]], [[:en:1870|1870]]), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the [[:en:Tokyo Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace (Tokyo)]]) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the [[:en:Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|University of Tokyo Komaba Campus]]) to oversee [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:Military exercise|military exercise]]s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on [[:en:November|November]]'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is [[:en:Shibuya Hikarie|Shibuya Hikarie]]. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from [[:en:Shibuya Station|Shibuya Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852577]] Wikidata name: Mitake Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|en|Shibuya (district)|WD=Q595153|lt=Shibuya (district)}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} | founded = {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|en|April 17|WD=Q2520|lt=April 17}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3, {{ill|May 17|en|May 17|WD=Q2575|lt=May 17}}, {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace (Tokyo)}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|en|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|WD=Q7896466|lt=University of Tokyo Komaba Campus}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}} {{ill|Military exercise|en|Military exercise|WD=Q357104|lt=military exercise}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|en|Shibuya Hikarie|WD=Q11561670|lt=Shibuya Hikarie}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|en|Shibuya Station|WD=Q428811|lt=Shibuya Station}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|en|Shibuya (district)|WD=Q595153|lt=Shibuya (district)}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} | founded = {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|en|April 17|WD=Q2520|lt=April 17}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3, {{ill|May 17|en|May 17|WD=Q2575|lt=May 17}}, {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace (Tokyo)}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|en|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|WD=Q7896466|lt=University of Tokyo Komaba Campus}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}} {{ill|Military exercise|en|Military exercise|WD=Q357104|lt=military exercise}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|en|Shibuya Hikarie|WD=Q11561670|lt=Shibuya Hikarie}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|en|Shibuya Station|WD=Q428811|lt=Shibuya Station}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya|1=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|en|Shibuya (district)|WD=Q595153|lt=Shibuya (district)|1=Shibuya (district)}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} | founded = {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570|1=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki|1=Genki (era)}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya|1=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570|1=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki|1=Genki (era)}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|en|April 17|WD=Q2520|lt=April 17|1=April 17}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 3, {{ill|May 17|en|May 17|WD=Q2575|lt=May 17|1=May 17}}, {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|en|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|WD=Q7896466|lt=University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|1=Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}} {{ill|Military exercise|en|Military exercise|WD=Q357104|lt=military exercise|1=Military exercise}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November|1=November}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|en|Shibuya Hikarie|WD=Q11561670|lt=Shibuya Hikarie|1=Shibuya Hikarie}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|en|Shibuya Station|WD=Q428811|lt=Shibuya Station|1=Shibuya Station}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)]][[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitake Shrine (御嶽神社) | image = [[File:Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御岳神社) - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya|1=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Shibuya (district)|en|Shibuya (district)|WD=Q595153|lt=Shibuya (district)|1=Shibuya (district)}} 1-12-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|37.6|N|139|42|12.8|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} | founded = {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570|1=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki|1=Genki (era)}} 1) | alternate_names = Miyamasu Mitake Shrine (宮益御嶽神社), Shibuya Mitake Shrine (渋谷御嶽神社) }} [[File:Mitake jinjya-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii and haiden (distant view)]] [[File:Mitake Jinjya Shibuya Tokyo.jpg|thumb|250px|Approach extending from the southern {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}]] '''Mitake Shrine''' (御嶽神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Shibuya|en|Shibuya|WD=Q193638|lt=Shibuya|1=Shibuya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. The shrine is also called '''Miyamasu Mitake Shrine''' (宮益御嶽神社), taking its name from the nearby {{ill|Miyamasuzaka|ja|宮益坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka|1=Miyamasuzaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455210}}. The shrine is situated on the rooftop of a building housing the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center, facing south. A gently sloping staircase extends toward Miyamasuzaka in the south. == Overview == Mitake Shrine was founded in {{ill|1570|en|1570|WD=Q6659|lt=1570|1=1570}} ({{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|WD=Q1145064|lt=Genki|1=Genki (era)}} 1). Within the {{ill|shrine precincts|ja|境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precincts|1=keidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429002}} stands a monument inscribed with "Meiji Emperor Mitake Shrine Rest Stop Site." On {{ill|April 17|en|April 17|WD=Q2520|lt=April 17|1=April 17}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 3, {{ill|May 17|en|May 17|WD=Q2575|lt=May 17|1=May 17}}, {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}), {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} took a rest stop at the shrine while traveling from the imperial castle (now the {{ill|Tokyo Imperial Palace|en|Tokyo Imperial Palace|WD=Q500681|lt=Imperial Palace (Tokyo)|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}}) to Komaba Military Training Grounds (now the {{ill|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|en|Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo|WD=Q7896466|lt=University of Tokyo Komaba Campus|1=Komaba Campus, University of Tokyo}}) to oversee {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}} {{ill|Military exercise|en|Military exercise|WD=Q357104|lt=military exercise|1=Military exercise}}s. The stone monument commemorates this honor as an "Imperial Site" (a location associated with Emperor Meiji) Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992, pp. 112-114. Every year, during the {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori no Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival held on {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November|1=November}}'s Tori days, a fair is hosted at the shrine. It is said that in years with a "San no Tori" (third Tori day), there is a higher likelihood of fires. As of April 12, 2023, a city planning decision was made to demolish the shrine as part of redevelopment {{Cite web |title=Shibuya Station East Exit "Miyamasuzaka District" Major Redevelopment City Planning Decision, Oyama Kaidō Reorganization |url=https://www.shibukei.com/headline/17432/ |website=Shibuya Keizai Shimbun |access-date=December 5, 2023}}. Opposition movements against the redevelopment, which includes Mitake Shrine ({{ill|Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|ja|宮益坂地区再開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamasuzaka District Redevelopment|qq=}}), have been taking place. == Gallery == mitake_miyamasu_1.jpg|First Torii mitake_miyamasu_2.jpg|Second Torii mitake_miyamasu_4.jpg|Komainu (left - ungyō form) mitake_miyamasu_3.jpg|Komainu (right - agyō form) mitake_miyamasu_5.jpg|View southward from the shrine grounds. The building in the background is {{ill|Shibuya Hikarie|en|Shibuya Hikarie|WD=Q11561670|lt=Shibuya Hikarie|1=Shibuya Hikarie}}. ontake_jinja_shibuya_north.jpg|Backside (north side) of the shrine. The building contains the Shibuya Ward Chamber of Commerce and Consumer Center. == Access == *3-minute walk from {{ill|Shibuya Station|en|Shibuya Station|WD=Q428811|lt=Shibuya Station|1=Shibuya Station}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == *Sato Noboru, "Shibuya-ku Historic Walks (Tokyo Historic Guide 13)," {{ill|Gakuseisha|ja|学生社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakuseisha|1=Gakuseisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448819}}, 1992. == External Links == * [https://shibuyamiyamasu.jp/?page_id=396 Miyamasu Mitake Shrine] - Shibuya Miyamasu Shopping Street Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|version=101570474|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] [[ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)|ja|ja:御嶽神社 (渋谷区)]] {{Commons category|Mitake-jinja (Shibuya, Tokyo)}} {{wikidata link|Q106852577}} [[Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Ontake Shrines|ja|Category:Ontake Shrines]] [[Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shibuya (district)]] [[Category:Shibuya|ja|Category:Shibuya]] [[Category:Shrines in Shibuya]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
